reason: While the text clearly portrays Moses and Aaron as leaders who communicate directly with God and perform the plagues, it does not establish that Hebrew leaders are more important than regular people. The Passover salvation narrative (Exodus 12) applies the blood protection equally to all Israelite households regardless of social status. The instructions 'let them take for themselves a lamb...each household' (12:3) establish universal application of the covenant and protection, with no indication of differential value or divine preference based on leadership status. The text shows role differentiation but not a hierarchy of importance or worth between leaders and people.
